# Scrimba Guide — Full Content Index

> Independent guide to Scrimba courses, learning paths, pricing, and comparisons. Written by Yassine El Haddad, a senior developer who completed 40+ Scrimba courses and all 4 career paths.

## About This Site

Scrimba Guide (scrimbaguide.tech) is an independent resource providing honest reviews, path breakdowns, pricing analysis, and platform comparisons for Scrimba — the interactive coding education platform. We are not affiliated with Scrimba. Some links are affiliate links (we may earn a commission at no extra cost to you).

### Author
- **Name:** Yassine El Haddad
- **Role:** Senior Developer & Independent Scrimba Reviewer
- **Credentials:** Completed 40+ Scrimba courses and all four career paths (Frontend, Fullstack, Backend, AI Engineer)
- **LinkedIn:** https://www.linkedin.com/in/yassine-el-haddad/
- **GitHub:** https://github.com/yel-hadd

## What is Scrimba?

Scrimba is an interactive coding education platform that teaches web development, AI engineering, and programming through a unique video-meets-IDE format called "scrims." Instead of passively watching tutorials, you can pause any lesson and edit the instructor's code directly in the browser. It offers 86+ courses across 4 career paths: Frontend Developer, Fullstack Developer, Backend Developer, and AI Engineer. The platform has a free tier with 19+ full courses and a Pro subscription for full access.

## Scrimba Pricing (2026)

- **Free tier:** 19+ full courses, no credit card required, never expires
- **Pro annual:** ~$24.50/month billed annually ($294/year)
- **Pro monthly:** Higher per-month rate
- **20% off:** Available through partner links (applied automatically at checkout)
- **Student discount:** Available with valid student email
- What Pro includes: All 86+ courses, 4 career paths, certificates, Discord community, unlimited challenges

## Learning Paths Overview

### Frontend Developer Path
- **Duration:** ~70 hours
- **Level:** Beginner
- **Best for:** Complete beginners with no coding experience
- **Topics:** HTML, CSS, JavaScript, React, career prep
- **Created with:** Mozilla MDN
- **URL:** https://scrimbaguide.tech/docs/paths/frontend-developer-path/

### Fullstack Developer Path
- **Duration:** ~108 hours
- **Level:** Beginner to Intermediate
- **Best for:** Frontend devs who want to add backend + AI skills
- **Topics:** Frontend + backend + databases + TypeScript + Next.js + AI engineering
- **URL:** https://scrimbaguide.tech/docs/paths/fullstack-developer-path/

### Backend Developer Path
- **Duration:** ~60 hours
- **Level:** Intermediate
- **Best for:** Developers already comfortable with frontend
- **Topics:** Node.js, Express, SQL, TypeScript, cybersecurity, DevOps
- **URL:** https://scrimbaguide.tech/docs/paths/backend-developer-path/

### AI Engineer Path
- **Duration:** ~40 hours
- **Level:** Intermediate to Advanced
- **Best for:** Developers who want to build AI-powered applications
- **Topics:** Agents, RAG, MCP, context engineering, Vercel AI SDK, multimodality
- **URL:** https://scrimbaguide.tech/docs/paths/ai-engineer-path/

## Key Comparisons

### Scrimba vs Codecademy
Scrimba's interactive video scrims vs Codecademy's text-based lessons. Scrimba wins for web dev and AI (pause + edit code in video). Codecademy wins for language variety (15+ languages, mobile app). Both similar pricing.
URL: https://scrimbaguide.tech/docs/comparisons/scrimba-vs-codecademy/

### Scrimba vs Udemy
Scrimba: curated subscription with interactive scrims. Udemy: marketplace with 200K+ courses. Scrimba better for structured paths. Udemy better for niche one-off topics.
URL: https://scrimbaguide.tech/docs/comparisons/scrimba-vs-udemy/

### Scrimba vs freeCodeCamp
Scrimba: interactive video + paid Pro tier. freeCodeCamp: 100% free, text-based. Scrimba for hands-on learners willing to pay. freeCodeCamp for disciplined self-learners on strict budget.
URL: https://scrimbaguide.tech/docs/comparisons/scrimba-vs-freecodecamp/

### Scrimba vs The Odin Project
Scrimba: guided interactive video. Odin: free, project-heavy, documentation-style. Both excellent. Scrimba for guided paths. Odin for self-directed learners.
URL: https://scrimbaguide.tech/docs/comparisons/scrimba-vs-odin-project/

### Scrimba vs Frontend Masters
Scrimba: beginner-to-job paths with interactive scrims. Frontend Masters: expert workshop depth with passive video. Scrimba for career path learners. FM for intermediate/advanced developers.
URL: https://scrimbaguide.tech/docs/comparisons/scrimba-vs-frontendmasters/

### Scrimba vs Boot.dev
Scrimba: interactive video for web dev + AI. Boot.dev: gamified backend CS fundamentals (Python, Go). Different specialties.
URL: https://scrimbaguide.tech/docs/comparisons/scrimba-vs-boot-dev/

### Additional Comparisons Available
- Scrimba vs Coursera: https://scrimbaguide.tech/docs/comparisons/scrimba-vs-coursera/

- Scrimba vs Fireship: https://scrimbaguide.tech/docs/comparisons/scrimba-vs-fireship/

## Frequently Asked Questions

### Is Scrimba good for beginners?
Yes, especially if you learn by doing. Scrimba's free tier includes 19+ courses. Start there, then commit to a career path when your consistency is proven.

### How much does Scrimba Pro cost?
Scrimba Pro costs approximately $24.50/month on an annual plan ($294/year). A 20% discount is available through partner links. The free tier never expires and includes 19+ full courses.

### Is Scrimba free to use?
Partially. About 19 full courses are free (labeled "Community"), including Learn React, Learn JavaScript, and Learn TypeScript. Career paths and advanced courses require Pro.

### Is Scrimba Pro worth it?
Usually yes for learners wanting structured career paths, access to all 86+ courses, and certificates. If uncertain, test with free courses first.

### Does Scrimba offer certificates?
Yes. Completing a career path earns a Certificate of Completion for LinkedIn and resumes. Individual course certificates are also available.

### Can Scrimba help me get hired?
It can improve portfolio and interview readiness significantly. Hiring depends on execution, applications, and market conditions. Career paths include job-search modules.

## Course Topics Covered
- **Frontend:** React, JavaScript, CSS, HTML, Tailwind, Vue
- **Backend:** Node.js, Express, SQL, NestJS, Firebase
- **AI & ML:** AI Agents, RAG, LangChain, OpenAI, MCP, Vercel AI SDK
- **Languages:** Python, TypeScript
- **Career:** Interview prep, portfolio building, getting hired
